scope_boundary: Within one finite sequential grant-to-effect model using trusted identities and receipts, all 38 authority mutations were rejected, two pre-effect denials remained observable, and zero governed unsafe releases occurred.
transition_reason: The exact subordinate invariant survived every declared widening mutation over the modeled grant, principal, operation, target, ceiling, epoch, expiry, remaining-use, approval, and dispatch surfaces.
negative_results: identities and receipts are trusted inputs
no deployed enforcement or distributed revocation
downgrade_triggers: trusted inputs described as authenticated enforcement
promotion_burden: Repeat with authenticated principals, trusted time, complete effect mediation, concurrent and partitioned revocation, live adapters, independent attacks, useful-throughput accounting, and transfer.
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; no external human review requested or claimed
non_claims: does not promote any chapter core claim
does not establish deployed authorization, complete mediation, security, distributed revocation, or open-world safety
does not establish SOTA, AGI, ASI, publication, release, or deployment readiness
